原文:
Easy Ways to Build Vocabulary

It's not all that hard to build an advanced① and large vocabulary. Like many things in life, it's an ongoing② process, and the best part of the process is that there's enough room for improvement③, which means you'll just keep getting better and better. Of course you have to work at it. You wouldn't think that a few months of exercise in your teens would be enough for the rest of your life, and that's also true for building your vocabulary—you have to keep at it daily, and pretty soon you will find that you have an excellent④ vocabulary.
翻译:
扩大词汇量的简单方法

建立一个高级且庞大的词汇量并非那么困难。就像生活中的许多事情一样，这是一个持续的过程，而这个过程最好的部分在于有足够的改进空间，这意味着你会变得越来越好。当然，你必须为此付出努力。你不会认为青少年时期的几个月锻炼就足以支撑余生，这对于扩大词汇量也同样适用——你必须每天坚持，很快你就会发现自己拥有了出色的词汇量。
必背词汇:
① advanced /ədˈvɑːnst/ adj. 高级的；先进的
Ⓡ advance /ədˈvɑːns/ v. 前进；促进 n. 进展
Ⓡ advantage /ədˈvɑːntɪdʒ/ n. 优势
Ⓡ disadvantage /ˌdɪsədˈvɑːntɪdʒ/ n. 劣势
② ongoing /ˈɒnɡəʊɪŋ/ adj. 持续的；进行中的
Ⓡ go on 继续
Ⓡ ongoing process 持续的过程
③ improvement /ɪmˈpruːvmənt/ n. 改进；改善
Ⓡ improve /ɪmˈpruːv/ v. 改进；提高
Ⓡ prove /pruːv/ v. 证明
④ excellent /ˈeksələnt/ adj. 出色的；极好的
Ⓡ excellence /ˈeksələns/ n. 卓越；优秀
Ⓡ excel /ɪkˈsel/ v. 擅长；胜过

原文: One of the effective⑤ ways to build vocabulary is to read good books. You need to really read at least one good book a week, preferably a classic⑥. This isn't as hard as it sounds, and it is far better than any other method because you improve your vocabulary while reading an interesting piece of literature⑦. Another nice thing is that you learn both new words and their use unconsciously⑧, meaning that you will tend to use the words learned/learnt this way in conversations almost automatically.
翻译: 扩大词汇量的有效方法之一是阅读好书。你需要真正每周至少读一本好书，最好是经典著作。这并不像听起来那么难，而且它远比其他任何方法都好，因为你在阅读有趣的文学作品的同时提高了词汇量。另一个好处是，你会在不知不觉中学习新单词及其用法，这意味着你几乎会自动地在对话中使用以这种方式学到的单词。
必背词汇:
⑤ effective /ɪˈfektɪv/ adj. 有效的；起作用的
Ⓡ effect /ɪˈfekt/ n. 影响；效果
Ⓡ affect /əˈfekt/ v. 影响
Ⓡ affection /əˈfekʃn/ n. 喜爱
⑥ classic /ˈklæsɪk/ n. 经典著作 adj. 经典的
Ⓡ classical /ˈklæsɪkl/ adj. 古典的
Ⓡ classify /ˈklæsɪfaɪ/ v. 分类
⑦ literature /ˈlɪtrətʃə(r)/ n. 文学；文献
Ⓡ literary /ˈlɪtərəri/ adj. 文学的
Ⓡ literate /ˈlɪtərət/ adj. 有读写能力的
⑧ unconsciously /ʌnˈkɒnʃəsli/ adv. 无意识地；不知不觉地
Ⓡ conscious /ˈkɒnʃəs/ adj. 有意识的
Ⓡ consciousness /ˈkɒnʃəsnəs/ n. 意识
